title: Adaptive basis enrichment for a variational bound-state method
gist: Extend a variational bound-state method with an adaptive rule that enriches the trial basis where a residual-based error indicator is largest, aiming at faster convergence of low-lying states with an explicit error indicator reported alongside each level.

claims.claim: Residual-based error indicators locate the regions where a variational trial basis is deficient.
A pilot computation shows the adaptive enrichment rule reduces the basis size needed for a fixed energy tolerance by roughly a factor of three on a benchmark family.
Greedy enrichment strategies are known to converge for the class of symmetric operators considered.

note: Synthetic example card for tests and protocol demonstrations. The references are placeholders, not real publications or real computations.
